result:
Confirmed — all occurrences are confined to `src/debug.c` and `src/zmalloc.c`. Here's the full report:

## `#ifdef USE_JEMALLOC` — Memory allocator subsystem

**4 occurrences**, all tied to jemalloc-specific tuning/introspection:

| File:Line | Context |
|---|---|
| `src/zmalloc.c:637` | `zfree_with_size()` — uses `je_sdallocx()` (jemalloc's sized-free, avoids an emap lookup) instead of the generic `zfree()` path when jemalloc is the active allocator |
| `src/debug.c:349` | Defines `mallctl_int()`, a helper that calls jemalloc's `mallctl()` to get/set integer allocator tuning parameters |
| `src/debug.c:460` | Adds `MALLCTL <key> [<val>]` and `MALLCTL-STR <key> [<val>]` to the `DEBUG HELP` command-usage text, only shown when jemalloc is present |
| `src/debug.c:1104` | In `debugCommand()` dispatch — wires up the `DEBUG MALLCTL` / `DEBUG MALLCTL-STR` subcommands to `mallctl_int`/`mallctl_string` |

**Subsystem:** memory allocator (`zmalloc` layer + `DEBUG` command's allocator-tuning introspection). This code exposes/uses jemalloc-specific APIs (`je_sdallocx`, `mallctl`) that only exist when Redis is built against jemalloc rather than libc malloc/tcmalloc.

## `#ifdef HAVE_BACKTRACE` — Crash/debug reporting subsystem

**10 occurrences**, all in `src/debug.c`, all related to generating stack traces on crash/assert/signal:

| Line | Context |
|---|---|
| 35 | Includes `<execinfo.h>` (and `<ucontext.h>`/OpenBSD shim) — the backtrace API headers |
| 1260 | In the assertion-failure handler — calls `logStackTrace(NULL, 1, 0)` before `printCrashReport()` |
| 1381 | In the panic handler — same `logStackTrace()` call on panic |
| 1408 | Opens a large block (paired with `bugReportStart`) defining `getAndSetMcontextEip()`, used to read/patch the crashed thread's instruction pointer from `ucontext_t` |
| 2111 | Guards the real `#define BACKTRACE_MAX_SIZE 100` and the platform-specific stack-trace collection code (vs. the `setupStacktracePipe()` no-op stub used when backtrace isn't available) |
| 2564 | In `sigsegvHandler()` — extracts `eip` via `getAndSetMcontextEip()` to log "Crashed running the instruction at: %p" |
| 2598 | Still in `sigsegvHandler()` — calls `dumpCodeAroundEIP(eip)` to disassemble/dump code near the crash address |
| 2752 | In `sigalrmSignalHandler()` — declares `ucontext_t *uc` (else just discards `secret`) |
| 2766 | Same handler — calls `logStackTrace(getAndSetMcontextEip(uc, NULL), 1, 0)`; falls back to "Sorry: no support for backtrace()." when unavailable |
| 2818 | Opens the `#ifdef __linux__` "Stacktrace Utils" section — `is_thread_ready_to_signal()` and related multi-thread stack-dumping helpers used by the crash handler to capture all threads' stacks |

**Subsystem:** crash/debug reporting. All of it lives in the SIGSEGV/SIGBUS/SIGALRM signal handlers, `bugReportStart`/`printCrashReport`, and the assert/panic paths — i.e., Redis's "print a stack trace and crash log when something goes catastrophically wrong" machinery, which depends on glibc's `backtrace()`/`backtrace_symbols()` from `execinfo.h` (unavailable on some platforms like musl-libc/Alpine).

**Summary:** the two guards are cleanly separated by subsystem and by file — `USE_JEMALLOC` lives in `zmalloc.c`/`debug.c`'s `DEBUG MALLCTL*` code (allocator internals), while `HAVE_BACKTRACE` is exclusively in `debug.c`'s signal-handler/crash-report code (crash diagnostics). No other file in `src/`, `modules/`, or `tests/` references either macro.
